source.raw_ocr_text: adj. (表达观点时 ) 尖锐的; MHI AB presenting a point of view, especially a controversial one, in an excessively and unpleasantly forceful way: strident critics 激烈的批评家

headline.core_cn: 刺耳的；咄咄逼人的；尖锐的
headline.core_en: loud, harsh, and forceful in sound or opinion

familiar_bridge.summary: strict 是严格；strident 不是严格，而是声音或观点太尖、太强硬，让人不舒服。

core.main: strident 可以形容刺耳声音，也常形容观点、语气、批评过于强硬。

senses.label: 刺耳的；尖锐强硬的
senses.text: 声音刺耳，或表达观点时过分强烈、咄咄逼人。

usage_groups.group_title: 声音或语气刺耳尖厉
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调 harsh sound。

examples.sentence: A strident alarm cut through the quiet hall.
examples.translation: 刺耳的警报声划破了安静的大厅。

usage_groups.group_title: 观点或批评过于强硬
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于政治、评论、争论等场景。

examples.sentence: The editorial adopted a strident tone toward the government’s policy.
examples.translation: 社论对政府政策采取了尖锐强硬的语气。
examples.teaching_note: strident tone 强调态度咄咄逼人。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The harsh sound made everyone wince.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 刺耳的声音让每个人都皱眉。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The loud protest interrupted the meeting.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 响亮的抗议打断了会议。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: loud 只说明音量大，不一定刺耳。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Her shrill voice carried across the room.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 她尖厉的声音传遍房间。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: shrill 更偏高而尖的声音。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The proposal met with vehement opposition.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这个提案遭到激烈反对。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: vehement 形容强烈情绪，不一定刺耳。

antonyms.example_sentence: He gave a mild response to the accusation.
antonyms.example_translation: 他对指控作出温和回应。
antonyms.difference_note: mild 与 strident 的强硬相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The speaker’s subdued tone calmed the crowd.
antonyms.example_translation: 演讲者克制的语气让人群平静下来。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The new law imposes stringent safety standards.
confusable_words.example_translation: 新法律施加严格安全标准。
confusable_words.difference_note: stringent 是规则严格；strident 是声音或观点尖锐。

extension.root_affix: strident 可和 shrill, harsh, vehement 放在一起辨析；不要把它和 stringent 混淆。

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 神气十足; 潇酒的气质 flamboyant confidence of style or manner: He rides with panache. 他总是神气活现地骑马 。 11S

headline.core_cn: 潇洒气派；神气十足的风格
headline.core_en: flamboyant confidence or stylish flair

familiar_bridge.summary: style 是风格；panache 是带自信和气派的风格，通常让人觉得漂亮、潇洒。

core.main: panache 描述做事或表现方式有 flair，不只是做成，而是做得有风度、有气场。

senses.label: 潇洒气派；神气十足
senses.text: 做事方式有自信、风格和吸引力。

usage_groups.group_title: 做事或表现有风格和气派
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调 stylish confidence。

examples.sentence: She delivered the difficult speech with panache.
examples.translation: 她潇洒自信地完成了那场难度很高的演讲。

examples.sentence: The designer turned a simple room into a space full of panache.
examples.translation: 设计师把简单的房间变成了很有气派的空间。
examples.teaching_note: 这里强调风格上的吸引力。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The pianist played with style.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 钢琴家演奏得很有风格。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She has a flair for dramatic entrances.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 她很会制造戏剧性的出场效果。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: flair 强调天赋和风格感。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The essay is written with verve.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这篇文章写得很有神采。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The violinist ended with a bravura performance.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 小提琴手以精彩炫技的表演收尾。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: bravura 更偏表演上的炫目技巧。

antonyms.example_sentence: The dullness of the presentation made the audience restless.
antonyms.example_translation: 陈述的乏味让听众坐立不安。

antonyms.example_sentence: His awkwardness made the entrance less impressive.
antonyms.example_translation: 他的笨拙让出场没那么有气势。

extension.root_affix: 学习重点是 with panache 这个表达。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.raw_ocr_text: #74 0X vt， 困扰; 使不安 cause distress or anxiety to: He was not troubled by doubts. 他没有被疑虑困扰 。 派生 troubling /trAblm/ adj. 令人不安的 causing distress or anxiety: troubling news 令人不安的

headline.core_en: to make someone worried or distressed

familiar_bridge.summary: trouble 作名词是“麻烦”；作动词时是“让某人心里不安、困扰”。

core.main: GRE 中 trouble 常形容某个问题、矛盾或现象让人难以安心。

senses.label: 使烦恼；使担忧
senses.text: 某事让人心里不安，觉得需要认真对待。

usage_groups.group_title: 问题或事实让人不安
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调 cause anxiety or concern。

examples.sentence: The inconsistency in the data troubled the researchers.
examples.translation: 数据中的不一致让研究人员感到担忧。

examples.sentence: She was troubled by the possibility that the witness had lied.
examples.translation: 她担心证人可能撒了谎。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The delay worried the passengers.
The noise bothered everyone in the room.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 噪音让房间里所有人烦恼。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: bother 更日常，也可指打扰。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The discovery disturbed the committee.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这个发现让委员会不安。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The report disquieted many observers.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这份报告让许多观察者不安。

antonyms.example_sentence: The explanation reassured the audience.
antonyms.difference_note: reassure 与 trouble 的使担忧相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her words comforted the frightened child.
antonyms.example_translation: 她的话安慰了受惊的孩子。

extension.root_affix: trouble 的动词义可和 troubled, troubling 一起记。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

需避免名词 trouble 抢重点

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 权宜之计; 应急的手段 a means of attaining an end, especially one that is convenient but considered improper or immoral: resort to many expedients to avoid bankruptcy 采取许多应急办法来避免破产 | Aspirin is useful expedient for dulling minor pain. >| a] LARARRRR 痛的有用应急药物

headline.core_cn: 权宜之计；方便但未必正当的
headline.core_en: a convenient means to an end, especially one chosen for practicality rather than principle

familiar_bridge.summary: expedite 是“加快”；expedient 关注“为了达成目的而方便可行”，但常带不够原则的味道。

core.main: expedient 可以作名词表示权宜之计，也可作形容词表示出于实际方便而采取的。

senses.label: 权宜之计
senses.text: 为了达到目的而临时采用的办法，可能方便但不够理想或不够正当。

senses.label: 方便可行的；权宜的
senses.text: 强调 practical, convenient rather than principled。

usage_groups.group_title: 临时可行但未必有原则的办法
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调 convenient means, not ideal solution。

examples.sentence: The tax cut was defended as a temporary expedient, not a permanent solution.
examples.translation: 减税被辩护为临时权宜之计，而不是永久解决方案。
examples.teaching_note: 名词 expedient 指临时办法。

usage_groups.group_title: 出于方便而做出的选择
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于形容决定、政策或行动偏实用而非原则。

examples.sentence: The leader found it expedient to delay the announcement until after the election.
examples.translation: 领导人觉得把公告推迟到选举后更方便可行。
examples.teaching_note: find it expedient to 表示出于现实考虑。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The shortcut saved time but created new problems.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 捷径节省了时间，却制造了新问题。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: shortcut 指节省时间的捷径；expedient 强调“暂时有用”，但可能不够稳妥或不够原则。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The emergency measure calmed the market.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: measure 中性，expedient 常有权宜味。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The tent served as a makeshift classroom.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 帐篷充当临时教室。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The subsidy was only a stopgap.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 补贴只是临时措施。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: stopgap 与 temporary expedient 很近。

antonyms.example_sentence: The judge acted on principle rather than convenience.
antonyms.example_translation: 法官按原则而非便利行事。
antonyms.difference_note: principle 与 expedient 的便利取向相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The plan was an ideal solution, not a mere expedient.
antonyms.example_translation: 这个计划是理想方案，不只是权宜之计。
antonyms.difference_note: ideal solution 对比权宜之计。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The agency promised an expeditious review.
confusable_words.example_translation: 机构承诺迅速审查。
confusable_words.difference_note: expeditious 是迅速；expedient 是权宜方便。

extension.root_affix: expedient 与 expedite / expeditious 形式相近，但意思不同：expedient 是权宜可行，expeditious 是迅速。

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 智者; 贤人; 哲人 a profoundly wise man, especially one who features in ancient history or legend: The sage is the instructor of a hundred ages. 这位哲人是百代之师

headline.core_en: a very wise person; wise and judicious

familiar_bridge.summary: wise 是“明智”；sage 既可指“智者”，也可作形容词表示“明智的”。

core.main: sage 常带古典、庄重感，指有深厚判断力的人或建议。

senses.label: 智者；贤人
senses.text: 因智慧和判断力而受尊敬的人。

senses.label: 明智的
senses.text: 形容 advice、counsel、decision 等有判断力。

usage_groups.group_title: 有智慧、受尊敬的人
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 带有古典或文学色彩。

examples.sentence: The king consulted an old sage before making the decision.
examples.translation: 国王在做决定前咨询了一位老智者。
examples.teaching_note: 名词 sage 指受尊敬的智者。

usage_groups.group_title: 建议或判断明智稳健
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里强调建议或判断明智稳健。

examples.sentence: Her sage advice prevented the committee from rushing into a costly mistake.
examples.translation: 她明智的建议避免委员会匆忙犯下代价高昂的错误。
examples.teaching_note: 形容词 sage 强调判断力。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: A wise person listens before speaking.
It was wise to wait for more evidence.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 等待更多证据是明智的。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The savant could explain the ancient text in detail.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这位博学者能详细解释古文。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The editor made a judicious selection of essays.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 编辑审慎地选择了文章。

antonyms.example_sentence: Only a fool would ignore such evidence.
antonyms.example_translation: 只有愚人才会无视这样的证据。

antonyms.example_sentence: It was unwise to dismiss the warning.

confusable_words.example_sentence: Sagebrush grows in dry regions.
confusable_words.example_translation: 蒿属植物生长在干旱地区。
confusable_words.difference_note: sagebrush 是植物名，不是智者义。

extension.root_affix: sage 作名词和形容词都很常见；可和 sagacious, wisdom, judicious 放在一起记。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. #485; 聪慧的 having or showing keen mental discernment and good judgment; shrewd: He was sagacious enough to back out. 他及时抽身退出 ， 真是英明 。 近义词 insightful

headline.core_en: showing keen judgment and practical wisdom

familiar_bridge.summary: sage 是智者；sagacious 是像智者一样有判断力，尤其能看清局势。

core.main: sagacious 不是单纯聪明，而是判断稳、看得准，常形容 leader、observer、decision。

senses.label: 睿智的；有判断力的
senses.text: 能准确判断形势，并作出明智选择。

usage_groups.group_title: 判断形势准确而明智
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调 good judgment, not just intelligence。

examples.sentence: The sagacious leader waited until public opinion had shifted before proposing reform.
examples.translation: 这位睿智的领导者等到舆论转变后才提出改革。
examples.teaching_note: sagacious leader 强调判断时机。

examples.sentence: Her sagacious analysis revealed a risk that others had missed.
examples.translation: 她睿智的分析揭示了别人忽略的风险。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: A wise decision can prevent later regret.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 明智的决定可以避免日后后悔。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The smart answer solved the immediate problem.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 聪明的回答解决了眼前问题。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: smart 偏聪明，sagacious 偏判断力。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The judge made a judicious ruling.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 法官作出了明智审慎的裁决。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: A discerning reader notices the irony.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 有眼光的读者会注意到讽刺。

antonyms.example_sentence: It was foolish to ignore the early warnings.
antonyms.example_translation: 忽视早期警告是愚蠢的。

antonyms.example_sentence: The shortsighted policy created future costs.
antonyms.example_translation: 短视政策造成了未来代价。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The sage offered practical advice.
confusable_words.example_translation: 智者给出了实际建议。
confusable_words.difference_note: sage 可作名词或形容词；sagacious 是形容词。

extension.root_affix: sagacious 与 sage, sagacity 是同一词族，可一起记成“智慧判断”。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 传染性的; 有感染力的 likely to spread or influence others in a rapid manner: an infectious laugh 有感染力的笑声

headline.core_en: able to spread to others, literally or emotionally

familiar_bridge.summary: infect 是感染；infectious 既可说疾病会传播，也可说笑声、热情会带动别人。

core.main: infectious 的底层感觉是 spread：从一个人或地方扩散到其他人。

senses.label: 传染性的
senses.text: 疾病、病菌等容易传播。

senses.label: 有感染力的
senses.text: 情绪、笑声、热情等让别人也受到影响。

usage_groups.group_title: 疾病或病菌会传播
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于医学和公共卫生语境。

examples.sentence: The hospital isolated patients with infectious diseases.
examples.translation: 医院隔离了患传染病的病人。

usage_groups.group_title: 情绪、笑声或热情会带动别人
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调一种状态很快影响周围人。

examples.sentence: Her infectious enthusiasm made the whole team more confident.
examples.translation: 她富有感染力的热情让整个团队更有信心。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The flu is highly contagious.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: contagious 与疾病和情绪都可搭。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: His excitement was catching.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Measles is a communicable disease.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 麻疹是一种可传播疾病。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Her smile had an irresistible warmth.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 她的微笑有一种难以抗拒的温暖。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 用于情绪感染力时可接近。

antonyms.example_sentence: The condition is painful but noninfectious.
The outbreak was contained within a week.
antonyms.example_translation: 疫情在一周内得到控制。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The infected wound needed treatment.
confusable_words.example_translation: 感染的伤口需要治疗。
confusable_words.difference_note: infected 是“已被感染”；infectious 是“会感染别人”。

extension.root_affix: infectious 来自 infect；-ious 常构成形容词。

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. 放弃; 抛弃 abandon (someone or something): Friends have forsaken her. KAU T th. 过去式 forsook

headline.core_en: to abandon someone, something, or a former way of life

familiar_bridge.summary: forsake 比 leave 更重，像把人、地方、原则或信仰抛在身后。

core.main: forsake 常带文学或正式色彩，强调背离原本承诺、归属或习惯。

senses.label: 抛弃；离弃
senses.text: 离开原本应陪伴、支持或坚持的人或事。

senses.label: 放弃
senses.text: 放弃一种做法、信念或生活方式。

usage_groups.group_title: 抛下人、地方或群体
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调 abandonment，有情感重量。

examples.sentence: She felt that her friends had forsaken her during the crisis.
examples.translation: 她觉得朋友们在危机中抛弃了她。
examples.teaching_note: forsake 比 simply leave 更带背弃感。

usage_groups.group_title: 放弃信念、原则或旧生活
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于 beliefs, principles, habits。

examples.sentence: The reformer refused to forsake his principles for political convenience.
examples.translation: 改革者拒绝为了政治方便而放弃原则。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The villagers abandoned the flooded homes.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 村民放弃了被洪水淹没的家园。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He left the city after the war.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 战后他离开了这座城市。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The soldier deserted his post.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 士兵擅离岗位。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: She renounced her claim to the throne.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 她放弃了王位继承权。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: renounce 强调公开正式放弃。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her colleagues supported her through the trial.
antonyms.example_translation: 同事们在审判期间支持她。

antonyms.example_sentence: The judge upheld the principle of fairness.

confusable_words.example_sentence: Few people foresaw the crisis.
confusable_words.example_translation: 很少有人预见到危机。
confusable_words.difference_note: foresee 形式相近但意思完全不同。

extension.root_affix: forsake 的过去式是 forsook，过去分词 forsaken；常见搭配是 be forsaken。

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 事后的领悟; 事后聪明 understanding of a situation or event only after it has happened or developed: By hindsight I should have gone there earlier. 事后想来 ， 我应早些去那里 。 | Hindsight is always clearer than one's view at the time. 事后看问题总要比当时看得清楚

headline.core_en: understanding an event only after it has happened

familiar_bridge.summary: hind 是“后面”；sight 是看见。hindsight 就是事情发生后才看清。

core.main: hindsight 常用于 in hindsight，表示现在回头看才明白，当时未必看得出。

senses.label: 事后认识
senses.text: 事情发生后才获得的理解。

usage_groups.group_title: 回头看才明白当时的情况
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 多出现在评价过去决策、错误或信号。

examples.sentence: In hindsight, the warning signs were obvious.
examples.translation: 事后看来，警告信号很明显。
examples.teaching_note: in hindsight 是最核心结构。

examples.sentence: With hindsight, the committee should have questioned the data more carefully.
examples.translation: 事后看来，委员会本该更仔细地质疑数据。
examples.teaching_note: with hindsight 用于回顾判断。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: In retrospect, the plan was too risky.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 回头看，这个计划风险太大。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: retrospect 与 hindsight 很接近。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The safety rule was added as an afterthought.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 安全规则是事后补上的。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: afterthought 是事后补充的想法。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Retrospection changed his view of the failure.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 回顾改变了他对失败的看法。

antonyms.example_sentence: Foresight allowed the city to prepare for floods.
antonyms.example_translation: 远见让城市为洪水做好准备。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her prescience about the market surprised investors.
antonyms.example_translation: 她对市场的预见让投资者惊讶。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The essay offers insight into political fear.
confusable_words.example_translation: 这篇文章提供了对政治恐惧的洞察。
confusable_words.difference_note: insight 不限于事后；hindsight 必须在事后。

extension.root_affix: hind 表示后面，sight 表示看见；hindsight 与 foresight 正好形成对照。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj，( 改革、 做法、 差异等 ) 彻底的; 根本上的 (especially of change or action) relating to or affecting the fundamental nature of something; far-reaching or thorough: a radical change A adj. ( 政治立场等 ) 激进的; 极端的 advocating or based on thorough or complete political or social reform; representing or supporting an extreme section of a political party: @ radical activist 极端的活动家 adj. 前卫的; 全新的; 前所未见的 characterized by independence of or departure from tradition; innovative or unorthodox: radical ideas about education 关于教育的全新看法 n. 激进分子; 极端分子 a person who advocates thorough or complete political or social reform: He was a radical when he was young. 他年轻时是个激进分子

headline.core_en: fundamental, far-reaching, or politically extreme

familiar_bridge.summary: radical 的底层不是“疯狂”，而是从 root 出发：触及根本，所以变化会很彻底。

core.main: radical 可形容根本性的改变，也可形容政治立场激进；阅读中要看它修饰 change 还是 ideology。

senses.label: 根本的；彻底的
senses.text: 触及事物基础结构或本质。

senses.label: 激进的
senses.text: 政治或社会观点远离主流、要求大幅改变。

usage_groups.group_title: 变化或改革触及根本
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调 far-reaching and fundamental。

examples.sentence: The proposal called for a radical reform of the school system.
examples.translation: 这项提案要求对学校制度进行根本性改革。

usage_groups.group_title: 政治或思想立场激进
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里强调政治或思想立场激进。

examples.sentence: The movement began with radical ideas about property and equality.
examples.translation: 这场运动起初提出了关于财产和平等的激进思想。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The basic problem has not changed.
The plan seemed extreme to moderate voters.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这个计划在温和选民看来很极端。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The discovery caused a fundamental change in the theory.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这个发现导致理论发生根本变化。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The invention had a revolutionary impact on communication.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这项发明对通信产生了革命性影响。

antonyms.example_sentence: The candidate took a moderate position on reform.
antonyms.example_translation: 候选人在改革上采取温和立场。

antonyms.example_sentence: The policy made only superficial changes.
antonyms.example_translation: 这项政策只作了表面改变。
antonyms.difference_note: superficial 对应根本义的反面。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The group became radicalized after years of conflict.
confusable_words.example_translation: 多年冲突后该组织变得激进化。
confusable_words.difference_note: radicalized 是状态变化，不等于 radical 的所有义项。

extension.root_affix: radical 来自 root 的概念；radix 表示根，所以 radical change 是触及根部的改变。

related_words.note: 也带 root 的“连根去除”意象。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 可怜的; 可悲的; 极为差劲的 to a very poor or inadequate degree: a pitiful amount of money 少得可怜的钱

headline.core_cn: 可怜的；可悲的；少得可怜的
headline.core_en: deserving pity or so poor as to be contemptible

familiar_bridge.summary: pity 是怜悯；pitiful 可以说人处境可怜，也可以说数量、表现差到可怜。

core.main: pitiful 有两个常见方向：让人同情，或差得让人失望。

senses.label: 可怜的；令人同情的
senses.text: 处境悲惨，让人产生怜悯。

senses.label: 差劲得可怜的
senses.text: 数量、质量或表现低到令人失望。

usage_groups.group_title: 处境可怜、令人同情
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调 deserving pity。

examples.sentence: The abandoned dog looked pitiful in the rain.
examples.translation: 那只被遗弃的狗在雨中显得很可怜。

usage_groups.group_title: 数量或表现差到可怜
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调 very poor or inadequate。

examples.sentence: The company offered a pitiful amount of compensation.
examples.translation: 公司给出的赔偿少得可怜。
examples.teaching_note: 这里是程度低、令人不满。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: It was a sad story about loss.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这是一个关于失去的悲伤故事。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The poor creature had nowhere to hide.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这个可怜的生灵无处躲藏。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: poor 可指可怜或质量差。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His pathetic excuse convinced no one.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 他那可怜的借口没有说服任何人。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: pathetic 与 pitiful 很近，但可更贬义。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The refugees were in a pitiable condition.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 难民处境令人怜悯。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her admirable courage inspired the class.
antonyms.example_translation: 她令人钦佩的勇气鼓舞了全班。

antonyms.example_sentence: The team made an impressive recovery.
antonyms.example_translation: 团队取得了令人印象深刻的恢复。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The dictator showed pitiless cruelty.
confusable_words.example_translation: 独裁者表现出无情的残忍。
confusable_words.difference_note: pitiless 是没有怜悯，不是可怜。

extension.root_affix: pitiful 来自 pity；pitiless 是“没有怜悯的”，方向相反。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 平等主义的; 主张人人平等的 relating to or believing in the principle that all people are equal and deserve equal rights and opportunities: a fairer, more egalitarian society 一个更加公平 、 平等的社会

headline.core_cn: 平等主义的；主张平等的人
headline.core_en: believing that all people deserve equal rights and opportunities

familiar_bridge.summary: equal 是平等；egalitarian 是把平等当成原则或制度目标。

core.main: egalitarian 常修饰 society、principle、policy，强调减少等级差异、机会更平等。

senses.label: 平等主义的
senses.text: 相信人应享有平等权利和机会。

senses.label: 平等主义者
senses.text: 支持平等原则的人。

usage_groups.group_title: 社会、制度或政策强调平等
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于讨论权利、机会、等级结构。

examples.sentence: The reform was designed to create a more egalitarian education system.
examples.translation: 这项改革旨在建立一个更加平等的教育体系。
examples.teaching_note: egalitarian education system 强调机会更平等。

examples.sentence: Her politics are egalitarian, emphasizing equal access to health care.
examples.translation: 她的政治立场主张平等，强调医疗资源的平等获得。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The law promises equal treatment for all citizens.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 法律承诺所有公民受到平等对待。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: A fair system gives everyone a chance.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 公平制度给每个人机会。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The group used a democratic process to choose its leader.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这个团体用民主程序选择领导者。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: democratic 有时接近平等参与。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy aims at an equitable distribution of resources.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 政策旨在公平分配资源。

antonyms.example_sentence: The company has a strongly hierarchical culture.
antonyms.example_translation: 这家公司有强烈的等级文化。

antonyms.example_sentence: Critics called the admissions policy elitist.
antonyms.example_translation: 批评者称招生政策是精英主义的。
antonyms.difference_note: elitist 强调少数精英优先。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The essay uses equalitarian as a less common variant.
confusable_words.example_translation: 文章把 equalitarian 用作较少见的变体。
confusable_words.difference_note: equalitarian 少见，egalitarian 更常用。

extension.root_affix: egalitarian 与 equality, equal 放在一起记；egalitarianism 是“平等主义”。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 中世纪的 relating to the Middle Ages: a medieval castle 中世纪的城堡

headline.core_cn: 中世纪的；过时而野蛮的
headline.core_en: relating to the Middle Ages; sometimes old-fashioned or brutal

familiar_bridge.summary: medieval 首先是历史时期“中世纪的”；有时也借来批评某做法落后、野蛮。

core.main: 阅读中要判断它是历史描述，还是带评价色彩的比喻。

senses.label: 中世纪的
senses.text: 与欧洲中世纪有关。

senses.label: 落后野蛮的
senses.text: 比喻某制度或做法像旧时代一样残酷或过时。

usage_groups.group_title: 描述中世纪历史或文化
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于历史、建筑、文学、制度。

examples.sentence: The museum displays medieval manuscripts from the twelfth century.
examples.translation: 博物馆展出十二世纪的中世纪手稿。

usage_groups.group_title: 批评做法过时或野蛮
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里强调批评做法过时或野蛮。

examples.sentence: Critics called the prison conditions medieval.
examples.translation: 批评者称监狱条件落后而野蛮。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The old wall still surrounds the town.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: old 只是旧，不等于中世纪。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The ancient road crossed the desert.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The village retained feudal social relations.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 村庄保留了封建社会关系。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: feudal 常与中世纪制度相关。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The law uses archaic language.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这部法律使用古旧语言。

antonyms.example_sentence: Modern medicine changed life expectancy.
antonyms.example_translation: 现代医学改变了预期寿命。

antonyms.example_sentence: The gallery focuses on contemporary art.

confusable_words.example_sentence: The middle-aged teacher returned to school.
confusable_words.example_translation: 那位中年教师回到学校。
confusable_words.difference_note: middle-aged 是年龄，不是中世纪。

extension.root_affix: medieval 与 Middle Ages 直接相关；mediaeval 是另一种拼法。

word_family.meaning_cn: 中世纪主义；中世纪风格

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 指示者; 指示物 a thing, especially a trend or fact, that indicates the state or level of something: This index is a really good indicator of the way the economy works. 这一指数能充分说明经济的运行情况 。 9 indicative /m'dikastrv/ adj. 表明 … 的 serving as a sign or indication of something: A headache is sometimes indicative of eyestrain. £87 tt A HRA J. | Indicative of this change is the appearance of the two books under review. 这里所评论的两部作品的出现 ， 正好说明了这种变化

headline.core_en: a sign, measure, or fact that shows a condition or trend

familiar_bridge.summary: indicate 是“表明”；indicator 就是用来判断某种状态的迹象或指标。

core.main: indicator 常用于经济、科学、社会研究，表示某个数据或事实能指向更大的状态。

senses.label: 指标；迹象
senses.text: 帮助判断状态、水平或趋势的东西。

usage_groups.group_title: 数据或事实显示某种状态
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调 a sign that points to a larger condition。

examples.sentence: Unemployment is an important indicator of economic health.
examples.translation: 失业率是经济健康状况的重要指标。

examples.sentence: Early test scores are not always a reliable indicator of future success.
examples.translation: 早期考试分数并不总是未来成功的可靠指标。
examples.teaching_note: reliable indicator 常用于评价证据可靠性。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Dark clouds are a sign of rain.
The drop in sales was a signal of trouble.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 销售下滑是出现问题的信号。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The index measures consumer confidence.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这个指数衡量消费者信心。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Attendance can be a gauge of public interest.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 出席人数可以衡量公众兴趣。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The indicator is not necessarily the cause of the problem.
confusable_words.example_translation: 指标不一定是问题的原因。
confusable_words.difference_note: indicator 只是显示状态，不一定造成状态。

extension.root_affix: indicator 来自 indicate；-or 表示执行或承担功能的东西。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 无可置疑的; 无可指责的 not able to be doubted, questioned, or criticized; entirely trustworthy: a man of unimpeachable character 品格完美无缺的人 | information from an unimpeachable source 来源可靠的消息 i X44 blameless

headline.core_cn: 无可指摘的；无可置疑的
headline.core_en: so reliable or blameless that it cannot reasonably be questioned

familiar_bridge.summary: impeach 是质疑或弹劾；unimpeachable 就是经得住质疑，名誉或证据都很可靠。

core.main: unimpeachable 常修饰 integrity、source、logic、reputation，强调不能合理地怀疑或批评。

senses.label: 无可指摘的；无可置疑的
senses.text: 可靠、正直或严密到很难被质疑。

usage_groups.group_title: 名誉、品格或证据经得起质疑
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调 beyond reasonable challenge。

examples.sentence: The historian relied on documents from an unimpeachable source.
examples.translation: 历史学家依靠来自可靠无可质疑来源的文件。
examples.teaching_note: unimpeachable source 表示来源非常可信。

examples.sentence: Her unimpeachable integrity made the accusation seem absurd.
examples.translation: 她无可指摘的正直让指控显得荒唐。
examples.teaching_note: 这里强调品格无可批评。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The witness seemed trustworthy.
The data came from a reliable survey.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His conduct was irreproachable throughout the inquiry.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 整个调查中他的行为无可指责。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The evidence was incontrovertible.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 证据无可争辩。

antonyms.example_sentence: The report relied on questionable sources.
The claim rested on dubious assumptions.
antonyms.example_translation: 这个主张建立在可疑假设上。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The official’s conduct was considered impeachable.
confusable_words.example_translation: 这名官员的行为被认为可被弹劾。
confusable_words.difference_note: un- 使方向相反。

extension.root_affix: un- 是否定；impeach 可指质疑或弹劾；-able 表示可被。unimpeachable 就是不可被合理质疑。

source.raw_ocr_text: RSL v.t. *RGH; 接受 ( 权力或责任等 ) take or begin to have (power or responsibility): He is ready to assume control of the organization. 他准备好了掌管组织 。 近义词 shoulder

headline.core_en: to take something as true, take on a role, or begin to have a form

familiar_bridge.summary: assume 有 take 的感觉：把某事当真、把责任接过来，或呈现某种形式。

core.main: assume 是多义高频词：可以表示“假定某事为真”，也可以表示“承担责任/职位”，还可以表示“呈现某种形式或重要性”。

senses.label: 假定
senses.text: 没有完全证明就先当作真实。

senses.label: 承担；接任
senses.text: 开始拥有权力、责任或职位。

senses.label: 呈现；具有
senses.text: 某事逐渐表现出某种形式、性质或重要性。

usage_groups.group_title: 把某事当作真实前提
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调在证据尚未完全证明前，把某个前提暂时当真。

examples.sentence: The argument assumes that consumers always act rationally.
examples.translation: 这个论证假定消费者总是理性行动。
examples.teaching_note: assume that 表示“把后面的内容当作前提”。

usage_groups.group_title: 承担责任、权力或职位
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调接过某种责任、权力或正式职位。

examples.sentence: The new director will assume responsibility for the project next month.
examples.translation: 新主任下个月将承担这个项目的责任。

usage_groups.group_title: 呈现某种形式或重要性
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调某事逐渐具有某种形式、性质或重要性。

examples.sentence: The minor dispute soon assumed greater significance.
examples.translation: 这场小争端很快具有了更大的重要性。
examples.teaching_note: assume significance 表示变得重要。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: I suppose the train has already left.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: suppose 对应假定某事为真。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She took on new duties after the promotion.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 升职后她承担了新职责。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The law presumes innocence until guilt is proven.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 法律在定罪前推定无罪。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: presume 更正式，常有依据或制度前提。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The team undertook a difficult investigation.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 团队承担了一项困难调查。

antonyms.example_sentence: Researchers verified the claim with new data.
antonyms.example_translation: 研究人员用新数据核实了这一说法。
antonyms.difference_note: verify 与未证先假定相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The king relinquished power.

confusable_words.example_sentence: The machine consumes less energy than older models.
confusable_words.example_translation: 这台机器比旧型号耗能更少。
confusable_words.difference_note: consume 形式有 -sume，但意义不同。

extension.root_affix: assume, consume, presume, resume 都含有 -sume 这一“取、拿”的历史来源；学习时重点区分各自现代用法。

word_family.note: 同源连接，但现代义不同。

related_words.note: 假定某事为真的反向动作。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 不自大的; 谦逊的 not pretentious or arrogant; modest: an unassuming man —/ iif #84 A. 3344) modest

headline.core_en: modest and not trying to impress others

familiar_bridge.summary: assume 有“摆出某种样子”的感觉；unassuming 就是不摆架子、不刻意显得重要。

core.main: unassuming 形容人、态度、风格低调自然，没有自大或炫耀。

senses.label: 谦逊的；不张扬的
senses.text: 有能力或身份却不刻意表现自己。

usage_groups.group_title: 人或风格低调不炫耀
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调 modest without self-display。

examples.sentence: Despite his fame, the scientist remained unassuming and approachable.
examples.translation: 尽管很有名，这位科学家仍然谦逊而容易接近。
examples.teaching_note: unassuming 常形容有成就却不摆架子的人。

examples.sentence: The restaurant looks unassuming from the street, but its food is excellent.
examples.translation: 这家餐馆从街上看很不起眼，但菜品很好。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She gave a modest account of her achievements.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 她谦虚地讲述了自己的成就。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He remained humble after winning the prize.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The hotel is simple and unpretentious.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这家酒店简单而不装腔。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Her self-effacing humor made the audience like her.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 她不突出自己的幽默让观众喜欢她。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: self-effacing 更强调把自己放低。

antonyms.example_sentence: The arrogant official ignored every complaint.
antonyms.example_translation: 傲慢的官员无视每个投诉。

antonyms.example_sentence: The essay sounded pretentious rather than insightful.
antonyms.example_translation: 这篇文章听起来装腔作势，而不是有洞见。
antonyms.difference_note: pretentious 强调刻意显得高级。

extension.root_affix: un- 表示否定；unassuming 与 assuming 的“自以为是、摆架子”形成对照。

source.raw_ocr_text: vt. 养育; 培育; 教养 care for and encourage the growth or development of: These delicate plants need careful nurturing. 这些脆弱的植物需要精心培育

headline.core_en: to care for and encourage growth or development

familiar_bridge.summary: nature 是天性；nurture 是后天培养和照料。

core.main: nurture 既可养育孩子或植物，也可培养 talent、idea、relationship 等抽象事物。

senses.label: 养育；照料
senses.text: 照顾生命成长。

senses.label: 培养；培育
senses.text: 让能力、想法或关系逐渐发展。

usage_groups.group_title: 照料生命让其成长
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于 children, plants, animals。

examples.sentence: The program helps parents nurture their children in a safe environment.
examples.translation: 这个项目帮助父母在安全环境中养育孩子。

usage_groups.group_title: 培养能力、想法或关系
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于 talent, idea, relationship, habit。

examples.sentence: A good teacher can nurture curiosity rather than merely deliver facts.
examples.translation: 好老师能培养好奇心，而不只是传授事实。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: They raised three children in a small town.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 他们在小镇养育了三个孩子。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The course develops students’ writing skills.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 课程培养学生写作技能。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy fosters cooperation among schools.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这项政策促进学校之间的合作。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: foster 与 nurture 都可用于抽象发展。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The school cultivates independent thinking.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 学校培养独立思考。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: cultivate 更像有意识地培育。

antonyms.example_sentence: The garden died because it was neglected.
antonyms.example_translation: 花园因疏于照料而枯死。

antonyms.example_sentence: Rigid rules can stifle creativity.
antonyms.example_translation: 僵硬规则会压制创造力。
antonyms.difference_note: stifle 对应培养抽象能力的反面。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The debate contrasts nature with nurture.
confusable_words.example_translation: 这个争论把天性与后天培养相对照。
confusable_words.difference_note: nature 是天性，nurture 是培养。

extension.root_affix: nurture 常和 nature 对照：nature 是先天，nurture 是后天培养。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 衷心的; 诚挚的; 发自内心的 (of a feeling or its expression) sincere; deeply and strongly felt: heartfelt joy HRA HX | heartfelt thanks Hus th Rik i444 sincere

headline.core_en: sincere and deeply felt

familiar_bridge.summary: heart 是内心；heartfelt 就是从心里发出来的，不是表面客套。

core.main: heartfelt 常修饰 apology、thanks、sympathy、plea 等情感表达。

senses.label: 衷心的；诚挚的
senses.text: 感情真切，不是形式化表达。

usage_groups.group_title: 感谢、道歉或同情发自内心
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调 sincere feeling behind words。

examples.sentence: She sent a heartfelt apology after realizing how much harm her words had caused.
examples.translation: 意识到自己的话造成了多大伤害后，她发出了诚挚的道歉。

examples.sentence: The family expressed heartfelt thanks to the doctors who saved their son.
examples.translation: 这家人向救回儿子的医生表达了衷心感谢。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He gave a sincere apology.
Her concern seemed genuine.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 她的关心看起来是真诚的。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The student made an earnest request for help.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 学生诚恳地请求帮助。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The audience showed unfeigned admiration.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 观众表现出真诚的赞赏。

antonyms.example_sentence: The apology sounded insincere.
He gave a perfunctory thanks and left.

extension.root_affix: heartfelt = heart + felt，字面就是“心里感受到的”。

source.raw_ocr_text: n. (ded (2445EH9 ) 配偶 a wife, husband, or companion, in particular the spouse of a reigning monarch: The queen attended the opening of the exhibition with her consort. 女王和二夫一起参加了开幕式

headline.core_en: a spouse or companion; to associate with someone, often disapprovingly

familiar_bridge.summary: consort 作名词可指王室配偶；作动词 consort with 常带负面，表示和某类人混在一起。

core.main: 它的两个常见用法差异明显：一个是正式的“配偶”，一个是动词短语“与……交往”。

senses.label: 配偶；伴侣
senses.text: 尤其指君主的配偶。

senses.label: 与……交往；厮混
senses.text: 常指与不受欢迎的人来往。

usage_groups.group_title: 王室或正式语境中的配偶
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里强调王室或正式语境中的配偶。

examples.sentence: The queen consort accompanied the king at the ceremony.
examples.translation: 王后陪同国王出席典礼。
examples.teaching_note: 名词 consort 常见于王室语境。

usage_groups.group_title: 与某些人来往，常带贬义
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 动词结构是 consort with。

examples.sentence: The official was criticized for consorting with known criminals.
examples.translation: 这名官员因与已知罪犯来往而受到批评。
examples.teaching_note: consort with 常带负面色彩。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her spouse attended the event with her.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 她的配偶陪她参加了活动。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He associated with influential writers.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 他与有影响力的作家交往。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The traveler needed a reliable companion.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 旅人需要可靠同伴。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: companion 比 consort 更普通。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Soldiers were warned not to fraternize with the enemy.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 士兵被警告不要与敌人亲近交往。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: fraternize 常带不该交往的意味。

antonyms.example_sentence: She avoided people who might damage her reputation.
antonyms.example_translation: 她避开可能损害她名声的人。

antonyms.example_sentence: The two groups remained separate throughout the talks.
antonyms.example_translation: 谈判期间两组始终分开。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The musicians played in concert.
confusable_words.example_translation: 音乐家们协同演奏。
confusable_words.difference_note: concert 拼写接近但不同词。

extension.root_affix: consort 的名词义和动词短语 consort with 要分开记。

word_family.note: 来自 consortium，形式相近但不是本词常用派生。

source.raw_ocr_text: ie n. ”相似的事物; 与 … 相当的事物 a person or thing that is similar or analogous to another: a challenge that has no parallel in peacetime this century 本世纪和平时期从未有过的挑战 35044] counterpart n. 相似性; 相似之处 a similarity or comparison: There are many parallels between the stories. 这些故事之间存在很多相似性 。 vt， 与 … 相呼应;与 … 类似 be similar or corresponding to (something): Their test results parallel our own. 他们的结果和我们的一致 。 派生 unparalleled /an'peraleld/ adj. 无比的; 无双的; 独一无二的 having no parallel or equal; exceptional: an unparalleled success 空前的成就 | unparalleled difficulty 无比的困难 #5044) unprecedented

headline.core_cn: 相似事物；平行的；与……相似
headline.core_en: a similarity or correspondence between two things; running side by side

familiar_bridge.summary: parallel 本来是“平行”；抽象用法里指两件事结构相似，可以拿来对照。

core.main: parallel 可作名词表示“相似之处”，也可作形容词或动词表示并行、对应。

senses.label: 相似之处；对应关系
senses.text: 两件事在结构或发展上相似。

senses.label: 平行的；并行的
senses.text: 两条线或两件事并排进行。

usage_groups.group_title: 两件事之间有相似或对应
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 多出现在比较历史、案例、论证。

examples.sentence: The author draws a parallel between the two revolutions.
examples.translation: 作者把两场革命作了类比。
examples.teaching_note: draw a parallel 是核心表达。

usage_groups.group_title: 事物并行发展或平行存在
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于 lines, trends, processes。

examples.sentence: The two roads run parallel to each other for several miles.
examples.translation: 两条路有好几英里彼此平行。

examples.sentence: The study found parallel developments in art and politics.
examples.translation: 研究发现艺术和政治中出现了并行发展。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: There is a clear similarity between the two stories.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这两个故事之间有明显相似点。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The comparison helps explain the argument.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这个比较有助于解释论证。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The analogy between the brain and a computer is limited.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 大脑与计算机的类比是有限的。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The correspondence between theory and data was weak.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 理论与数据之间的对应关系很弱。

antonyms.example_sentence: The contrast between the two cities is striking.
antonyms.example_translation: 两座城市之间的差异很明显。
antonyms.difference_note: contrast 与 parallel 的相似对应相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The divergence between their views widened.
antonyms.example_translation: 他们观点之间的分歧扩大了。

confusable_words.example_sentence: A parallelogram has two pairs of parallel sides.
confusable_words.example_translation: 平行四边形有两组平行边。
confusable_words.difference_note: 数学词，不要和抽象 parallel 混。

extension.root_affix: parallel 与 analogy, correspondence 一起记，重点是“并排/对应”。

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. 使困惑; {HEY puzzle, confuse, or bewilder (someone): He seems truly bemused. 他看上去真的糊涂了

headline.core_en: to confuse or puzzle someone

familiar_bridge.summary: bemuse 和 confuse 接近，但常带“有点发愣、摸不着头脑”的感觉。

core.main: bemuse 常以 bemused 形式出现，形容人被某事弄得困惑、茫然。

senses.label: 使困惑；使茫然
senses.text: 让人一时不明白发生了什么。

usage_groups.group_title: 让人困惑或茫然
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 常见形式是 或

examples.sentence: The audience seemed bemused by the speaker’s sudden change of topic.
examples.translation: 演讲者突然转话题，听众似乎有些茫然。

examples.sentence: He gave a bemused smile when asked about the rumor.
examples.translation: 被问到传闻时，他露出茫然的微笑。
examples.teaching_note: bemused smile 表示困惑中带一点发愣。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The complex instructions confused the students.
The strange result puzzled the researchers.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 奇怪结果让研究人员迷惑。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The sudden reversal bewildered the audience.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 突然反转让观众茫然。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The contradiction perplexed the committee.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 矛盾让委员会费解。

antonyms.example_sentence: The chart clarified the argument.
The lecture enlightened many listeners.
antonyms.example_translation: 讲座让许多听众明白了。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The joke amused the children.
confusable_words.example_translation: 笑话逗乐了孩子们。
confusable_words.difference_note: amuse 是逗乐；bemuse 是困惑。

extension.root_affix: bemuse 与 amuse 形式相近但不同；bemused 是最常见形容词形式。

source.raw_ocr_text: #74)##X vt， 反对; 质疑; 提出异议 oppose (an action, decision, or theory) as mistaken or wrong: The losing candidate is contesting the results of the election. 失利的候选人质疑选举结果 。 派生 uncontested /'ankon'testid/ adj. 无争议的; 无人反对的 not contested: These claims have not gone uncontested. 这些说法并非未受质疑

headline.core_en: to challenge something as wrong; a competition

familiar_bridge.summary: contest 作名词是比赛；作动词重音常在后，表示质疑、争辩某个决定或说法。

core.main: GRE 常用 contest a claim / decision，重点是 challenge its validity。

senses.label: 质疑；争辩
senses.text: 认为某个说法、决定或结果不对，因此提出挑战。

senses.label: 比赛；竞赛
senses.text: 竞争活动。

usage_groups.group_title: 质疑说法、决定或结果
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调正式提出异议，认为某个说法、决定或结果不应被直接接受。

examples.sentence: Several scientists contested the claim that the data proved causation.
examples.translation: 几位科学家质疑“数据证明因果关系”这一说法。
examples.teaching_note: contest a claim 是核心动词用法。

examples.sentence: The losing candidate contested the election results.
examples.translation: 落败候选人对选举结果提出异议。
examples.teaching_note: contest results 表示挑战结果。

usage_groups.group_title: 比赛或竞赛
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 作名词时表示比赛、竞赛，重点是多人争夺一个结果或奖项。

examples.sentence: The essay contest attracted students from across the country.
examples.translation: 作文比赛吸引了全国各地的学生。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The lawyer challenged the witness’s account.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 律师质疑了证人的说法。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The report questions the old assumption.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The authors dispute the conventional explanation.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 作者们反驳传统解释。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The article impugned the senator’s motives.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 文章质疑参议员的动机。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: impugn 更强，常质疑动机或诚信。

antonyms.example_sentence: The court accepted the evidence.
He conceded defeat after the final count.
antonyms.example_translation: 最终计票后他承认失败。
antonyms.difference_note: concede 是停止争辩并承认。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The competition lasted three days.
confusable_words.example_translation: 比赛持续了三天。
confusable_words.difference_note: competition 对应名词义，不是质疑义。

extension.root_affix: contest 的动词和名词重音不同；学习重点是 contest a claim / decision。

source.raw_ocr_text: vt. AES; 削弱; 损害 to diminish or destroy by degrees: Rising prices might erode purchasing power. 正在上涨的价格可能会破坏购买力 。 | Business confidence is badly eroded, 经营的信心已大大减退 。 WR erosion /1'rauzan/ n. 削弱; 减少; 损害 the gradual destruction or diminution of something: the erosion of one's influence and power 某人的影响和权力的减弱 | erosion of real earnings by inflation 通货膨胀造成的实际收入的减少

headline.core_en: to wear away physically or gradually weaken

familiar_bridge.summary: erode 本来是水、风把土石慢慢磨掉；抽象里是信任、权力、支持一点点被削弱。

core.main: erode 的关键词是 gradually：不是一下摧毁，而是慢慢损耗。

senses.label: 侵蚀
senses.text: 风、水等慢慢磨损土地或表面。

senses.label: 逐渐削弱
senses.text: 信任、权威、权利等一点点减少。

usage_groups.group_title: 自然力量慢慢侵蚀表面
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于 soil, coast, rock, surface。

examples.sentence: Heavy rain eroded the hillside and exposed the roots of the trees.
examples.translation: 大雨侵蚀了山坡，露出了树根。

usage_groups.group_title: 抽象力量逐渐削弱信任或权力
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里强调抽象力量逐渐削弱信任或权力。

examples.sentence: Repeated scandals eroded public confidence in the agency.
examples.translation: 一再发生的丑闻逐渐削弱了公众对该机构的信心。
examples.teaching_note: erode confidence 是常见抽象用法。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The river wore away the soft rock.
The conflict weakened the alliance.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The scandal undermined the mayor’s authority.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 丑闻削弱了市长权威。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: undermine 与抽象 erode 接近。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Salt water corroded the metal pipes.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 盐水腐蚀了金属管道。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: corrode 更偏化学腐蚀，也可抽象。

antonyms.example_sentence: The agreement strengthened public trust.
The reforms restored confidence in the courts.
antonyms.example_translation: 改革恢复了人们对法院的信心。
antonyms.difference_note: restore 对应被侵蚀后的修复。

extension.root_affix: erode 的名词是 erosion；抽象搭配常是 erode trust / confidence / authority。

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. (#5; 利用 make use of: employ a hammer to drive a nail Fi #41 | The police employed force to break up the crowd. 警察使用暴力驱散群众

headline.core_en: to hire someone or use something for a purpose

familiar_bridge.summary: employ 不只是“雇人”，也常正式表示“使用某种方法、策略或工具”。

core.main: 读到 employ 要看宾语：employ workers 是雇用；employ a method 是运用。

senses.label: 雇用
senses.text: 让某人为机构工作。

senses.label: 使用；运用
senses.text: 为某目的采用方法、策略或资源。

usage_groups.group_title: 雇用人员
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于公司、机构和工作人员。

examples.sentence: The company employs more than five hundred workers.
examples.translation: 这家公司雇用了五百多名工人。

usage_groups.group_title: 使用方法、策略或工具
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 正式写作中很常见。

examples.sentence: The author employs irony to expose the weakness of the argument.
examples.translation: 作者运用讽刺来揭示论证的弱点。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The teacher used a diagram to explain the idea.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 老师用图表解释这个想法。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The firm hired two new engineers.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 公司雇用了两名新工程师。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The study utilized data from several countries.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 研究利用了多个国家的数据。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The writer deploys vivid imagery in the final paragraph.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 作者在最后一段运用生动意象。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: deploy 更强调有策略地使用。

antonyms.example_sentence: The manager dismissed the employee after repeated absences.
antonyms.example_translation: 经理因员工多次缺勤而解雇了他。

antonyms.example_sentence: The team discarded the old method.

confusable_words.example_sentence: Each employee received safety training.
confusable_words.example_translation: 每位雇员都接受了安全培训。
confusable_words.difference_note: employee 是被雇用的人。

extension.root_affix: employ 的名词和派生很多：employee 是雇员，employer 是雇主，employment 是就业或使用。

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 起源; 由来 the origin and the history of something: the pedigree of an idea 一个想法的由来 i 044 origin

headline.core_en: ancestry, origin, or a record of background and quality

familiar_bridge.summary: pedigree 原指动物的血统记录；后来也可说人、思想、机构的来历和背景。

core.main: pedigree 强调 origin and history，常用来说明某物为什么值得信任或有某种身份。

senses.label: 血统；谱系
senses.text: 动物或家族的祖先记录。

senses.label: 来历；背景资历
senses.text: 某人、机构、思想或作品的来源和历史。

usage_groups.group_title: 动物或家族的血统记录
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里强调动物或家族的血统记录。

examples.sentence: The breeder showed the buyer the dog’s pedigree.
examples.translation: 饲养者向买家展示了这只狗的血统记录。

usage_groups.group_title: 人、机构或思想的来历和资历
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 说明背景、传统或可信度。

examples.sentence: The theory has a long intellectual pedigree, reaching back to nineteenth-century philosophy.
examples.translation: 这个理论有悠久的思想谱系，可追溯到十九世纪哲学。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her background in biology helped the project.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 她的生物学背景帮助了项目。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The origin of the custom is uncertain.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这个习俗的起源不确定。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The family traced its lineage to a medieval ruler.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这个家族把血统追溯到一位中世纪统治者。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The museum verified the painting’s provenance.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 博物馆核实了这幅画的来源。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: provenance 常用于艺术品或文献来源。

antonyms.example_sentence: The manuscript emerged from obscurity only recently.
antonyms.example_translation: 这份手稿最近才从默默无闻中出现。
antonyms.difference_note: obscurity 可表示来源或名声不明。

confusable_words.example_sentence: A degree does not always prove intellectual pedigree.
confusable_words.example_translation: 学位并不总能证明思想背景。
confusable_words.difference_note: degree 是学位，不等于 pedigree 的来历资质。

extension.root_affix: 学习重点是从“血统记录”扩展到“思想、机构、作品的来历”。

word_family.meaning_cn: 有血统记录的；出身良好的

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. 0h; WES impose a misleading belief upon (someone); deceive; fool: She deluded me into thinking she was my friend. 她骗得我相信她是我的朋友 。 派生 delusive /drlu:srw adj. 虚假的; 欺骗性的 giving a false or misleading impression: delusive appearances 骗人的表象

headline.core_en: to make someone believe something false

familiar_bridge.summary: delude 不只是 lie，而是让人形成错误相信；也常说 delude oneself，自欺。

core.main: delude 的重点是 false belief：被骗后真的以为某事为真。

senses.label: 欺骗；使误信
senses.text: 让某人相信不真实的事。

senses.label: 自欺
senses.text: 让自己相信一个不愿面对的假象。

usage_groups.group_title: 让别人相信错误的事
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 常和 into doing / into thinking。

examples.sentence: The advertisement deluded customers into thinking the medicine had been proven effective.
examples.translation: 广告让顾客误以为这种药已经被证明有效。

usage_groups.group_title: 自欺，不愿面对现实
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 常用 delude oneself。

examples.sentence: He deluded himself into believing that the problem would disappear on its own.
examples.translation: 他自欺地相信问题会自行消失。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The forged letter deceived several officials.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 伪造信件欺骗了几名官员。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The trick fooled many viewers.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这个把戏愚弄了许多观众。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The chart misled readers about the scale of the change.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 图表误导了读者对变化规模的判断。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: mislead 强调导向错误理解。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Investors were duped by the false report.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 投资者被虚假报告欺骗。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: dupe 常指把人骗成受害者。

antonyms.example_sentence: The evidence enlightened the jury.
antonyms.example_translation: 证据让陪审团明白了真相。

antonyms.example_sentence: The teacher disabused them of the myth.
antonyms.example_translation: 老师纠正了他们对这个神话的误解。
antonyms.difference_note: disabuse 专指消除错误相信。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The answer eluded the researchers for years.
confusable_words.example_translation: 研究人员多年找不到答案。
confusable_words.difference_note: elude 是逃避或难以被找到，不是欺骗。

extension.root_affix: delude 的名词是 delusion；注意和 elude 区分。

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. 2818; 提纯 purify (a liquid) by vaporizing it, then condensing it by cooling the vapor, and collecting the resulting liquid: They managed to distill a small quantity of water. 他们成功蒸馆出了少量的水 。 vt， 提炼 (… 的精华 ); 浓缩 extract the essential meaning or most important aspects of: Her wisdom is distilled from many years of experience. 她的智慧提炼自多年的经验 。 KZ distillation /drstrlerfan/ n. 41%; 提炼 a process like distillation; also an instance of such distilling: The film is a distillation of personal experiences. 这部影片是个人经验的浓缩

headline.core_en: to purify a liquid or extract the essential meaning

familiar_bridge.summary: distill 字面是把液体蒸馏提纯；抽象里是把复杂内容提炼成核心。

core.main: distill 有两个常见画面：实验/工业中的提纯，以及写作思考中的提炼精华。

senses.label: 蒸馏；提纯
senses.text: 通过蒸发和冷凝提纯液体。

senses.label: 提炼；概括精华
senses.text: 从复杂材料中提取核心意思。

usage_groups.group_title: 把液体蒸馏提纯
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里强调把液体蒸馏提纯。

examples.sentence: The lab distilled the liquid to remove impurities.
examples.translation: 实验室蒸馏这种液体以去除杂质。

usage_groups.group_title: 从复杂内容中提炼核心
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于 argument, idea, evidence, experience。

examples.sentence: The author distills a complex debate into three clear questions.
examples.translation: 作者把复杂争论提炼成三个清晰问题。
examples.teaching_note: 抽象用法很适合阅读写作。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The filter purifies drinking water.
The study extracts useful patterns from the data.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 研究从数据中提取有用模式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The editor condensed the report into a short memo.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 编辑把报告压缩成简短备忘录。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The team refined the theory after new experiments.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 团队在新实验后改进了理论。

antonyms.example_sentence: Adding water diluted the solution.
Extra details obscured the main point.

extension.root_affix: distill 的名词是 distillation；distilled water 是蒸馏水。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: v.i.&v.t. (使 ) 蒸发; 汽化 convert or be converted into vapor: Cold gasoline does not vaporize readily. 低温汽油不容易汽化

headline.core_en: to turn into vapor or make something turn into vapor

familiar_bridge.summary: vapor 是蒸气；vaporize 就是变成蒸气，也可夸张地表示被瞬间摧毁。

core.main: vaporize 的核心是从固体或液体变成气态；阅读中常见科学义。

senses.label: 蒸发；汽化
senses.text: 变成或使变成蒸气。

senses.label: 使瞬间消失或摧毁
senses.text: 比喻或科幻语境中表示完全消灭。

usage_groups.group_title: 液体或物质变成蒸气
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于物理、化学、自然过程。

examples.sentence: At high temperatures, the liquid begins to vaporize.
examples.translation: 在高温下，液体开始汽化。

usage_groups.group_title: 强力作用下瞬间消失或被摧毁
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于 laser, explosion, heat 等语境。

examples.sentence: The intense laser pulse vaporized the thin metal film.
examples.translation: 强烈的激光脉冲使那层薄金属膜汽化。
examples.teaching_note: 这里仍和“化为蒸气”有关。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Water evaporates quickly in dry air.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 水在干燥空气中蒸发很快。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The mist disappeared after sunrise.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Heat can volatilize certain chemicals.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 热量能使某些化学物质挥发。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The device atomizes the liquid into a fine mist.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 装置把液体雾化成细雾。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: atomize 是变成微粒，不完全等同汽化。

antonyms.example_sentence: Steam condenses on the cold glass.
The gas liquefies under pressure.
antonyms.example_translation: 这种气体在压力下液化。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Vapor rose from the hot spring.
confusable_words.example_translation: 热泉上升起蒸气。
confusable_words.difference_note: vapor 是名词，vaporize 是动词。

extension.root_affix: vaporize = vapor + -ize，表示“使成为蒸气”。

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: 474 4#X adj.( 数量、 程度等 ) 有限的; 不多的 (of an amount, rate, or level of something) relatively moderate, limited, or small: Employment growth was relatively modest. 就业率增长有限 。 adj. (住所等 ) 不奢侈的 (of a place in which one lives, eats, or stays) not excessively large, elaborate, or expensive: We had bought a modest house. 我们买了一座普通的房子

headline.core_en: limited in amount, not showy, or not boastful

familiar_bridge.summary: modest 有两条主线：数量不大，或者人不张扬。

core.main: 看它修饰 amount/increase 还是 person/house，就能判断是“有限”还是“谦逊朴素”。

senses.label: 有限的；不多的
senses.text: 数量、程度或规模不大。

senses.label: 谦虚的；朴素的
senses.text: 人不自夸，或事物不奢华。

usage_groups.group_title: 数量、增长或规模有限
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里强调数量、增长或规模有限。

examples.sentence: The policy produced only a modest increase in employment.
examples.translation: 这项政策只带来了有限的就业增长。

usage_groups.group_title: 人或风格谦虚朴素
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于 person, manner, home, lifestyle。

examples.sentence: Although she won the award, she remained modest about her achievement.
examples.translation: 尽管获了奖，她对自己的成就仍很谦虚。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The project made a small profit.
He remained humble after his success.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The study found a moderate improvement.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 研究发现有适度改善。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The professor was brilliant but unassuming.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这位教授才华出众但不张扬。

antonyms.example_sentence: The reform led to substantial savings.
antonyms.difference_note: substantial 与 modest amount 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: His boastful speech annoyed the audience.
antonyms.example_translation: 他自夸的发言惹恼了听众。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The candidate took a moderate position.
confusable_words.example_translation: 候选人采取温和立场。
confusable_words.difference_note: moderate 与 modest 近，但不表示谦虚。

extension.root_affix: modest 的名词是 modesty；immodest 是反向词。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. ”相似物; 类似情况 a person or thing seen as comparable to another: Munich was the nearest analogue to what was happening then. 慕尼黑事件与当时的情况最相似 。 也作 analog 派生 analogous /s'nzlagas/ adj. 相似的; 类似的 comparable in certain respects, typically in a way that makes clearer the nature of the things compared: two poems analogous in mood 氛围相似的两首诗 i044 comparable

headline.core_en: something comparable to another thing

familiar_bridge.summary: analogue 和 analogy 都和“类比、相似对应”有关；analogue 是那个可以拿来对应的东西。

core.main: analogue 常用于科学、历史或文化比较，表示在另一个系统中功能相似的对应物。

senses.label: 类似物；对应物
senses.text: 在另一个环境中与某物功能或结构相似的东西。

usage_groups.group_title: 在另一个系统中找到对应物
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于 science, history, literature, society。

examples.sentence: The researchers found a modern analogue of the ancient voting system.
examples.translation: 研究人员找到了古代投票制度的现代对应物。
examples.teaching_note: analogue of 表示类似对应。

examples.sentence: This chemical has no exact analogue in the natural world.
examples.translation: 这种化学物质在自然界没有完全对应物。
examples.teaching_note: no exact analogue 常用于科学语境。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The minister met her French counterpart.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 部长会见了法国对应职位的官员。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: There is a parallel between the two cases.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 两个案例之间有相似之处。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The phrase has no exact English equivalent.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这个短语没有完全对应的英语表达。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The study identified a neural correlate of fear.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 研究发现了恐惧的神经对应物。

antonyms.example_sentence: The copy lacks the power of the original.
antonyms.example_translation: 复制品缺少原作的力量。

antonyms.example_sentence: The result was an anomaly rather than an analogue.
antonyms.example_translation: 这个结果是异常现象，而不是对应物。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The analogy helps explain the theory.
confusable_words.example_translation: 这个类比有助于解释理论。
confusable_words.difference_note: analogy 是类比关系；analogue 是类似物。

extension.root_affix: analogue, analogy, analogous 同属“相似对应”词族。美式常写 analog。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.raw_ocr_text: adv. 未经正式流程地;立即地 in a summary manner; without the customary formalities: He was summarily executed. 他被当场处决了

headline.core_cn: 立即地；不经正式程序地
headline.core_en: immediately and without the usual formalities

familiar_bridge.summary: summary 是摘要；summarily 不是“总结地”这么简单，常表示没走完整程序就立刻处理。

core.main: summarily 常和 dismissed、rejected、executed 等动词搭配，语气偏正式甚至严厉。

senses.label: 立即地；不经正式程序地
senses.text: 没有通常的解释、审理或手续就快速处理。

usage_groups.group_title: 不经完整程序就迅速处理
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 多出现在正式、法律或组织语境。

examples.sentence: The court summarily dismissed the complaint for lack of evidence.
examples.translation: 法院因证据不足而直接驳回了申诉。
examples.teaching_note: summarily dismissed 表示快速、不进入完整审理。

examples.sentence: The proposal was summarily rejected before the committee heard any testimony.
examples.translation: 委员会还没听取任何证词，这项提议就被直接驳回。
examples.teaching_note: 这里强调没有充分程序。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The manager responded immediately.
The rumor spread quickly.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The request was peremptorily denied.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 请求被断然拒绝。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The meeting ended abruptly.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 会议突然结束。

antonyms.example_sentence: The complaint was formally reviewed.
antonyms.difference_note: formally 与不经正式程序相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The committee acted deliberately.

confusable_words.example_sentence: The report begins with a summary.
confusable_words.example_translation: 报告以摘要开头。
confusable_words.difference_note: summary 是名词/形容词；summarily 是副词。

extension.root_affix: summarily 与 summary 同源，但常用搭配是 summarily dismiss / reject。

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: v.i. 和谐; 协调 produce a pleasing visual combination: The new building does not harmonize with its surroundings. 新建筑和周边环境不协调

headline.core_en: to make things fit together or sound pleasant together

familiar_bridge.summary: harmony 是和谐；harmonize 就是让不同元素彼此配合。

core.main: harmonize 可说颜色声音协调，也可说法律、政策、观点变得一致。

senses.label: 协调；使一致
senses.text: 让不同事物彼此配合、不冲突。

senses.label: 配和声
senses.text: 音乐中与主旋律协调地唱或演奏。

usage_groups.group_title: 颜色、声音或元素搭配和谐
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于艺术、音乐和视觉组合。

examples.sentence: The warm colors harmonize with the wooden furniture.
examples.translation: 暖色调和木质家具很协调。
examples.teaching_note: harmonize with 表示搭配和谐。

usage_groups.group_title: 规则、政策或观点变得一致
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于制度和抽象关系。

examples.sentence: The agreement aims to harmonize safety standards across the region.
examples.translation: 该协议旨在统一整个地区的安全标准。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The curtains match the carpet.
The teams coordinated their schedules.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The theory reconciles two conflicting observations.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 该理论调和了两个相互冲突的观察。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy aligns with international standards.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 该政策与国际标准一致。

antonyms.example_sentence: The bright colors clash with the quiet design.
antonyms.example_translation: 鲜艳颜色与安静设计不协调。

antonyms.example_sentence: The two accounts conflict.

confusable_words.example_sentence: The choir sang in harmony.
confusable_words.example_translation: 合唱团和声演唱。
confusable_words.difference_note: harmony 是名词，harmonize 是动词。

extension.root_affix: harmonize 来自 harmony；-ize 表示“使成为某种状态”。

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. lf]; 支持;认可 to publicly or officially say that you support or approve of (someone or something): endorse one's proposal 赞成某人的提议 | endorse a presidential candidate 支持某一总统侯选人近义词 support, uphold

headline.core_en: to publicly approve or support someone or something

familiar_bridge.summary: endorse 比 support 更正式，常是公开表态、官方认可或名人背书。

core.main: endorse 可用于候选人、政策、产品、观点，也可指在支票背面签名。

senses.label: 公开支持；认可
senses.text: 公开或正式地表示赞成。

senses.label: 背书；代言
senses.text: 用自己的名声为产品或观点提供支持。

usage_groups.group_title: 公开支持候选人、政策或观点
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 多见于 politics, institutions, reports。

examples.sentence: The newspaper endorsed the candidate a week before the election.
examples.translation: 这家报纸在选举前一周公开支持该候选人。
examples.teaching_note: endorse a candidate 是政治语境常见。

usage_groups.group_title: 为产品、方案或文件背书认可
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里强调为产品、方案或文件背书认可。

examples.sentence: Several doctors endorsed the new screening method.
examples.translation: 几位医生认可了这种新的筛查方法。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Many voters support the reform.
The board approved the plan.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The union backed the proposal.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 工会支持该提案。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: back 更口语但常用于政治。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The treaty was ratified by parliament.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 条约由议会正式批准。

antonyms.example_sentence: The senator opposed the bill.
The party repudiated the extremist statement.
antonyms.example_translation: 该党否认并拒绝接受极端言论。
antonyms.difference_note: repudiate 更强，表示公开切割。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The old legal text uses indorse as a spelling variant.
confusable_words.example_translation: 旧法律文本把 indorse 当作拼写变体。
confusable_words.difference_note: indorse 是少见拼写。

extension.root_affix: endorsement 是名词，常表示公开支持、产品背书或认可。

source.raw_ocr_text: v.i. 424%; 足以 … to meet or satisfy a need; be sufficient: One example will suffice. 举一个例子就够了 。 | No words will suffice to convey his grief. #hiHHRGEAEALAERREAN.

headline.core_en: to be enough for a purpose

familiar_bridge.summary: sufficient 是“足够的”；suffice 是动词，表示“够用了”。

core.main: suffice 常用于 A will suffice 和 suffice it to say，语气比 be enough 更正式。

senses.label: 足够；足以
senses.text: 数量、证据或行动满足需要。

usage_groups.group_title: 某物足以满足当前需要
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 多出现在 formal writing。

examples.sentence: A brief explanation will suffice for now.
examples.translation: 目前一个简短解释就足够了。

examples.sentence: The evidence did not suffice to prove intent.
examples.translation: 这些证据不足以证明意图。
examples.teaching_note: suffice to do 表示足以做某事。

usage_groups.group_title: 引出简短说明
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 固定结构 suffice it to say。

examples.sentence: Suffice it to say that the experiment changed the direction of the field.
examples.translation: 只需说，这个实验改变了该领域的方向。
examples.teaching_note: suffice it to say 用来省略细节。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: One example is enough to show the problem.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 一个例子足以说明问题。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The answer satisfied the examiner.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The proposal meets the basic requirements.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 提案满足基本要求。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The evidence was adequate for a preliminary conclusion.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 证据足以得出初步结论。

antonyms.example_sentence: The explanation fell short of what the audience needed.
antonyms.example_translation: 解释没有满足听众需要。

antonyms.example_sentence: The evidence failed to support the claim.
antonyms.difference_note: fail to do 与 suffice to do 反向。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The funds are sufficient for the first phase.
confusable_words.example_translation: 资金足以支持第一阶段。
confusable_words.difference_note: sufficient 是形容词，suffice 是动词。

extension.root_affix: suffice 与 sufficient, insufficient 是一组；读到 suffice 要想到 be enough。

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 不足的; 不充分的 not enough; inadequate: He had insufficient sleep. 他睡眠不足 。 | an insufficient market 供应不足的市场 i044) meager

headline.core_en: not enough for a purpose

familiar_bridge.summary: sufficient 是足够；insufficient 就是数量、证据或理由达不到需要。

core.main: insufficient 常修饰 evidence、funds、time、attention，强调不够支撑某个结论或需求。

senses.label: 不足的；不充分的
senses.text: 未达到所需数量、程度或标准。

usage_groups.group_title: 证据、资源或时间不够
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于学术、法律、政策和日常判断。

examples.sentence: The claim was rejected because the evidence was insufficient.
examples.translation: 这个主张因证据不足而被驳回。

examples.sentence: Insufficient funding forced the program to reduce its services.
examples.translation: 资金不足迫使该项目减少服务。
examples.teaching_note: insufficient funding 表示资源不够。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: There was not enough time to finish the test.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 没有足够时间完成考试。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The response was inadequate.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The diet was deficient in essential vitamins.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这种饮食缺乏必要维生素。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The report provided scant evidence.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 报告提供的证据很少。

antonyms.example_sentence: The evidence was sufficient to support the claim.
The team had ample time to prepare.
antonyms.difference_note: ample 比 sufficient 更充裕。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The old system was inefficient.
confusable_words.example_translation: 旧系统效率低下。
confusable_words.difference_note: inefficient 是效率低，不是数量不足。

extension.root_affix: in- 表示否定；insufficient 与 suffice, sufficient 同组。
